summary: Spolsky reflects on the declining effectiveness of advertising, noting that consumers have built up immunity to ads because advertisers repeatedly lied. He argues this forces companies into editorial and PR placement, where consumer defenses are lower. However, he identifies a tragedy of the commons: just as advertising destroyed its own credibility through overuse and dishonesty, editorial content will suffer the same fate once marketers pollute it with promotional messaging disguised as genuine content.
key_points: Traditional advertising has lost effectiveness because consumers learned not to trust it after years of dishonest ads
Companies are forced to infiltrate editorial content (product placements, PR) where audiences are less guarded
This represents a tragedy of the commons: each medium loses credibility as marketers exploit the trust it holds
The same cycle that destroyed advertising credibility will inevitably destroy editorial credibility too
money_quotes: If you have a product to sell you have no choice but to get into the editorial side, where consumers' defenses are lowered.
When advertising first rose to prominence, advertisements did work. We hadn't built up our immunities yet.
As more and more advertisers used the opportunity of the medium to lie, we learned not to trust advertisements.
Once editorial gets polluted by desperate marketers using PR instead of advertising to promote their message, nobody will believe it either.
key_insight: Advertising is a tragedy of the commons where each medium's trustworthiness is destroyed as marketers exploit it, and editorial content is next in line for the same fate.
